mnemonics.title: TYN for Typename
mnemonics.content: TYN = Tell Your compiler 'Yes' it's a Name that is a Type. Remember: when accessing nested types, you need T-Y-N to tell the compiler to treat it as a Type.

mnemonics.title: DEPENDS for Dependent Names
mnemonics.content: DEPENDS = Dependent Elements Parse Only When Explicitly Needed, i.e., compiler doesn't know if T::X is type or value until you specify with 'typename'.

mnemonics.title: EXPORT = EXternal PORT
mnemonics.content: Think of 'export' as trying to create an EXTERNAL PORT for templates, but it failed because most compilers couldn't build the 'port' (implementation was too difficult).

acronyms.usage: Used in templates to declare that a dependent nested name is a type, not a static member
Was intended for separate compilation of template definitions, now deprecated

acronyms.expansion: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
acronyms.usage: Template metaprogramming principle that allows selective template instantiation
A parameter that is itself a template, e.g., template<typename T, template<typename> class Alloc>

visualTips.visualization: Think of :: (scope resolution) as a door, and 'typename' as the key that opens it to reveal a type behind the door. Without the key, the compiler can't open the door to see if it's a type or value.

Imagine a factory (compiler) that receives blueprints (templates) but can't build the products separately - the factory needed special infrastructure that was too expensive. So templates ended up being built on-demand at each location (inclusion model).

analogies.analogy: Like asking a tour guide to clarify whether 'Washington' is a city (type) or a person (value) when referring to 'Washington Street'. The 'typename' keyword is that clarification.
Like calling someone at home - you need to know their name (the type) but you can't determine it until you know which person (template parameter) you're calling.
Like trying to build cars in separate factories and ship parts - the logistics were too complex, so car manufacturers build complete units at one location (header-only model).
